enriched_points.expanded_content: Traditional bridge monitoring relies heavily on manual inspections, which can miss subtle defects and require skilled personnel for accurate assessments.
Manual methods restrict data collection to periodic assessments, leading to gaps in monitoring and an inability to detect real-time structural changes.
The process of manual inspections is labor-intensive and costly, involving significant time for setup, execution, and reporting, ultimately impacting bridge maintenance budgets.
